title: 147 W Wood St, West Lafayette, IN 47906, USA | 4 Bedroom
description:
My apartment at EVER West Lafayette is a spacious 4-bedroom, 4-bath unit designed for both comfort and convenience. The community’s 4-bedroom layouts range from about 1,300 to 1,323 square feet, and each apartment is built with a modern student-living setup that makes sharing the space easy while still giving everyone privacy.

As a first-floor unit with a balcony, the apartment has a really nice mix of accessibility and indoor-outdoor living. EVER highlights private balconies as a feature of its townhome-style layouts, along with spacious kitchens and living rooms, which adds an open, social feel to the space.

Inside, the apartment is fully furnished and comes with features that make everyday living easy, including a 65-inch smart TV, hardwood-style plank flooring, quartz countertops, designer cabinets with soft-close drawers, stainless steel appliances, roller shades, and a full-size in-unit washer and dryer.

One of the best parts of living in a 4-person unit at EVER is that each resident has their own private bedroom and bathroom, so you get the social side of having roommates without giving up personal space. The apartment also includes keyless entry and USB outlets in both the kitchen and bedroom desks, which makes it feel especially practical for student life.

Contact me at 419-463-3025
or
Caillauxgustavo@gmail.com

description: Spacious pre-furnished apartment with a full bathroom and a separate kitchen/living room with a TV. The lease was originally $1699 per month, but I managed to get a deal that knocked off $465, bringing it down to $1234 per month. description:
Looking for a friendly, clean, and welcoming environment? This fully furnished private room in a 3-bedroom, 3-bath apartment is perfect for a female student! You’ll be sharing the apartment with two super-friendly female college students who keep the space always clean and organized.

Your room comes with a full-sized bed, a spacious desk for studying, a sliding-door closet, a dresser, and a private restroom with lots of counter space. I’ve installed my own showerhead with 7 different settings that softens hard water for a spa-like experience. You’ll also have a plate, utensil, and pot set, so you won’t need to buy any kitchen basics.

The apartment itself is modern and fully equipped, featuring air conditioning, dishwasher, microwave, stainless steel appliances, full-size washer and dryer, wood-style plank flooring, high-speed internet, and a private covered balcony.

The community amenities are fantastic: friendly, responsive staff, award-winning management, resort-style pool, basketball and tennis courts, dog park, outdoor game park, hiking trail, clubhouse with coffee bar, private study areas, and a 24-hour game & entertainment lounge. The apartment is just steps from Walmart Supercenter (10 min walk) and Celery Bog Nature Area (2 min walk) with beautiful trails. The nearest bus stop is at Walmart, with several bus routes providing easy access. Several restaurants and food spots are also within walking distance.

Flexible Summer Option: I’m happy to cover part of the rent over the summer if you won’t be living here, making it an even better deal.

This is a lease reassignment, so you’ll take over the remainder of my lease in a smooth process handled by the apartment office. description:
Hi

I’m looking for someone to sublease my apartment at Lark Townhomes between July 2025 and July 2026.

Lark Townhomes is a great complex with amenities like a gym, clubhouse, and pool. An exclusive city bus runs directly from the apartments to campus. Additionally, it’s just five minutes away from Walmart and Meijer.

The unit is a 4-bedroom townhouse, with each bedroom having a private bathroom. There’s an additional bathroom for guests, plus a washer and dryer in the unit. The kitchen is fully equipped with an oven, fridge, and microwave, and there’s a TV included. Free parking is also available.

The rent is $725/month plus $50–$60 for utilities. If you were to rent directly through Lark, the cost would be $850/month.

Lucas Florez.
